# Preflight Validation Script — Specification
**Project:** vexos-nix — Personal NixOS Flake  
**Spec Author:** Research Subagent  
**Date:** 2026-03-23  
**Target File:** `scripts/preflight.sh`

---

## 1. Purpose

This document specifies a CI-aligned preflight validation script for the **vexos-nix** NixOS Flake repository. The script must be run from the repository root on the NixOS host before any changes are committed or pushed to GitHub. It acts as the local enforcement gate that mirrors the standards checked by the CI/CD system.

---

## 2. Project Context

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Project type | Personal NixOS Flake |
| Flake target | `.#vexos` |
| NixOS release | 24.11 |
| Build command | `sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#vexos` |
| Test command | `nix flake check` + `sudo nixos-rebuild dry-build --flake .#vexos` |
| Key config file | `configuration.nix` |
| Hardware config | `/etc/nixos/hardware-configuration.nix` (host-only, NOT in repo) |
| Lock file | `flake.lock` (must be committed) |

---

## 3. Script Identity

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Path | `scripts/preflight.sh` |
| Shell | Bash — `#!/usr/bin/env bash` |
| Error handling | `set -euo pipefail` |
| Execution context | Run from repository root |
| File permissions | Executable (`chmod +x scripts/preflight.sh`) |

---

## 4. Output Conventions

All output uses ANSI color codes for terminal clarity:

| Symbol | Color | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `✓ PASS` | Green (`\033[0;32m`) | Check succeeded |
| `✗ FAIL` | Red (`\033[0;31m`) | Hard failure — script exits 1 |
| `⚠ WARN` | Yellow (`\033[0;33m`) | Advisory — script continues |

Color detection: use `tput colors` or check `$TERM` to fall back gracefully if no color support is present. Wrap color in a helper function.

---

## 5. Exit Code Policy

| Scenario | Exit Code |
|---|---|
| All hard checks pass (warns allowed) | `0` |
| Any hard failure (checks 1–4) | `1` |
| Warn-only failures (checks 5–8) | `0` (script continues) |

The script MUST NOT use `set -e` to silently abort on warn-only checks. Each check must be evaluated independently with explicit exit code capture.

---

## 6. Checks — Full Specification

### 6.1 CHECK 1 — Flake Structure Validation (HARD)

**Purpose:** Confirm the flake is syntactically valid and all outputs evaluate without error.

**Command:**
```bash
nix flake check
```

**Pass criteria:** Exit code `0`  
**Fail criteria:** Any non-zero exit code  
**Severity:** HARD — exits script with code `1` on failure  
**Output on pass:** `✓ PASS  nix flake check`  
**Output on fail:** `✗ FAIL  nix flake check — flake evaluation failed`  

**Notes:**
- Must be run without `sudo` (nix flake check does not require elevated privileges)
- On NixOS, the nix binary is always available on PATH
- If the Nix daemon is not running this will fail; that is intentional

---

### 6.2 CHECK 2 — Dry-Build System Closure (HARD)

**Purpose:** Verify the full NixOS system configuration can be built without actually switching. This catches missing packages, broken module options, and evaluation errors that `nix flake check` does not exercise.

**Command:**
```bash
sudo nixos-rebuild dry-build --flake .#vexos
```

**Pass criteria:** Exit code `0`  
**Fail criteria:** Any non-zero exit code  
**Severity:** HARD — exits script with code `1` on failure  
**Output on pass:** `✓ PASS  nixos-rebuild dry-build`  
**Output on fail:** `✗ FAIL  nixos-rebuild dry-build — system closure build failed`  

**Notes:**
- Requires `sudo` because `nixos-rebuild` accesses `/etc/nixos` and the Nix store
- The `--flake .#vexos` flag must match the flake output name exactly
- This check will also catch `/etc/nixos/hardware-configuration.nix` missing from the host; that is expected behavior

---

### 6.3 CHECK 3 — hardware-configuration.nix Not Tracked in Git (HARD)

**Purpose:** Prevent host-specific, auto-generated hardware configuration from being committed to the repository. This file is generated by `nixos-generate-config` and is unique per machine.

**Command:**
```bash
git ls-files hardware-configuration.nix
```

**Pass criteria:** Command produces empty output (file is not tracked)  
**Fail criteria:** Command produces any output (file name appears in output)  
**Severity:** HARD — exits script with code `1` on failure  
**Output on pass:** `✓ PASS  hardware-configuration.nix not tracked in git`  
**Output on fail:** `✗ FAIL  hardware-configuration.nix is tracked in git — remove it immediately`  

**Notes:**
- Run from repository root; `git ls-files` reports relative paths
- Also check `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard hardware-configuration.nix` to catch untracked files that are staged via `git add`
- Optionally verify `.gitignore` contains `hardware-configuration.nix` as an advisory

---

### 6.4 CHECK 4 — system.stateVersion Present in configuration.nix (HARD)

**Purpose:** Ensure `system.stateVersion` has not been accidentally removed from `configuration.nix`. This value controls NixOS stateful data paths and must never be absent or changed after initial installation.

**Command:**
```bash
grep -q 'system\.stateVersion' configuration.nix
```

**Pass criteria:** Exit code `0` (pattern found)  
**Fail criteria:** Exit code `1` (pattern not found)  
**Severity:** HARD — exits script with code `1` on failure  
**Output on pass:** `✓ PASS  system.stateVersion present in configuration.nix`  
**Output on fail:** `✗ FAIL  system.stateVersion missing from configuration.nix`  

**Notes:**
- Use `grep -q` (quiet mode) so no output is printed for the match itself
- The regex `system\.stateVersion` matches the literal attribute name with an escaped dot
- This check does not validate the value — only presence

---

### 6.5 CHECK 5 — Flake Lock Freshness (WARN)

**Purpose:** Warn when `flake.lock` has not been updated in more than 30 days, indicating pinned inputs may be significantly outdated (security updates, bug fixes missed).

**Command:**
```bash
LOCK_DATE=$(git log -1 --format="%ci" -- flake.lock | cut -d' ' -f1)
LOCK_EPOCH=$(date -d "$LOCK_DATE" +%s)
NOW_EPOCH=$(date +%s)
AGE_DAYS=$(( (NOW_EPOCH - LOCK_EPOCH) / 86400 ))
```

**Pass criteria:** `$AGE_DAYS` ≤ 30  
**Warn criteria:** `$AGE_DAYS` > 30  
**Severity:** WARN — script continues with exit `0`  
**Output on pass:** `✓ PASS  flake.lock updated ${AGE_DAYS} day(s) ago`  
**Output on warn:** `⚠ WARN  flake.lock is ${AGE_DAYS} days old — consider running: nix flake update`  

**Notes:**
- If `git log` returns empty output (file was never committed), emit WARN: `flake.lock has no git history`
- `date -d` (GNU coreutils) is available on NixOS; macOS compatibility is NOT required
- This check is informational only and must never block the build

---

### 6.6 CHECK 6 — Nix Formatting (WARN)

**Purpose:** Enforce consistent Nix code formatting across all tracked `.nix` files using `nixpkgs-fmt`. If the formatter is not installed, emit a warning and skip rather than hard-fail.

**Command sequence:**
```bash
if command -v nixpkgs-fmt > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  nixpkgs-fmt --check .
else
  # warn and skip
fi
```

**Pass criteria:** `nixpkgs-fmt --check .` exits `0` (all files formatted)  
**Warn criteria (format issues):** `nixpkgs-fmt --check .` exits non-zero  
**Warn criteria (not installed):** `nixpkgs-fmt` not on PATH  
**Severity:** WARN — script continues with exit `0` in all cases  
**Output on pass:** `✓ PASS  nixpkgs-fmt: all .nix files are correctly formatted`  
**Output on warn (format):** `⚠ WARN  nixpkgs-fmt: formatting issues found — run: nixpkgs-fmt .`  
**Output on warn (missing):** `⚠ WARN  nixpkgs-fmt not installed — formatting check skipped`  

**Notes:**
- `nixpkgs-fmt` can be installed via `nix shell nixpkgs#nixpkgs-fmt` or added to `environment.systemPackages`
- `--check` flag performs a dry-run diff without modifying files
- Alternatively, `alejandra --check .` is an acceptable substitute; prefer `nixpkgs-fmt` as the first option

---

### 6.7 CHECK 7 — No Hardcoded Secrets (WARN)

**Purpose:** Detect common patterns that suggest secrets have been hardcoded into tracked Nix files. This is a heuristic check and produces warnings rather than hard failures (false positives are possible).

**Patterns to check (grep extended regex):**
```
password\s*=\s*"[^"]+"
privateKey\s*=\s*"[^"]+"
AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}
[aA][pP][iI][-_]?[kK][eE][yY]\s*=\s*"[^"]+"
secret\s*=\s*"[^"]+"
token\s*=\s*"[^"]+"
```

**Command:**
```bash
TRACKED_NIX=$(git ls-files '*.nix')
grep -rEn \
  'password\s*=\s*"[^"]+"|privateKey\s*=\s*"[^"]+"|AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|[aA][pP][iI][-_]?[kK][eE][yY]\s*=\s*"[^"]+"|secret\s*=\s*"[^"]+"|token\s*=\s*"[^"]+"' \
  $TRACKED_NIX 2>/dev/null || true
```

**Pass criteria:** No matches found  
**Warn criteria:** One or more matches found  
**Severity:** WARN — script continues with exit `0`  
**Output on pass:** `✓ PASS  No hardcoded secrets detected in tracked .nix files`  
**Output on warn:** `⚠ WARN  Possible hardcoded secrets found — review the following lines:`  
  _followed by matching lines printed to stdout_  

**Notes:**
- Use `git ls-files '*.nix'` to restrict scope to tracked Nix files only
- Patterns like `hashedPassword` (using `mkpasswd`) are acceptable and will not match these patterns
- Known false positives: `services.openssh.settings` with `PermitRootLogin`, cachix public keys
- This check intentionally errs on the side of over-reporting; manual review is required

---

### 6.8 CHECK 8 — Flake Inputs Pinned (flake.lock Committed) (WARN)

**Purpose:** Confirm `flake.lock` exists and is tracked by git. An absent or uncommitted lock file means input versions are not reproducibly pinned, violating the Flake contract.

**Commands:**
```bash
# Check file exists on disk
test -f flake.lock

# Check file is tracked in git
git ls-files flake.lock
```

**Pass criteria:** File exists AND `git ls-files` returns non-empty output  
**Warn criteria:** File missing OR not tracked in git  
**Severity:** WARN — script continues with exit `0`  
**Output on pass:** `✓ PASS  flake.lock exists and is committed`  
**Output on warn (missing):** `⚠ WARN  flake.lock does not exist — run: nix flake lock`  
**Output on warn (untracked):** `⚠ WARN  flake.lock exists but is not tracked by git — run: git add flake.lock`  

**Notes:**
- `flake.lock` MUST be committed; it is not analogous to `package-lock.json` (which is sometimes gitignored)
- The warning severity is appropriate because a missing lock is likely caught by Check 1 (`nix flake check`) in any case

---

## 7. Script Skeleton Outline

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# =============================================================================
# preflight.sh — vexos-nix Pre-Push Validation Script
# Project: vexos-nix — Personal NixOS Flake (NixOS 24.11)
# Purpose: Validate flake structure, system closure, git hygiene, and code
#          quality before pushing changes to GitHub.
# Usage:   Run from repository root: bash scripts/preflight.sh
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# ---------- Color helpers -------------------------------------------------
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
RESET='\033[0m'

PASS() { echo -e "${GREEN}✓ PASS${RESET}  $1"; }
FAIL() { echo -e "${RED}✗ FAIL${RESET}  $1"; EXIT_CODE=1; }
WARN() { echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ WARN${RESET}  $1"; }

EXIT_CODE=0

# ---------- Header --------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "========================================================"
echo "  vexos-nix Preflight Validation"
echo "  $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')"
echo "========================================================"
echo ""

# ---------- CHECK 1: nix flake check -------------------------------------
echo "[1/8] Validating flake structure..."
if nix flake check; then
  PASS "nix flake check"
else
  FAIL "nix flake check — flake evaluation failed"
fi

# ---------- CHECK 2: nixos-rebuild dry-build -----------------------------
echo "[2/8] Verifying system closure (dry-build)..."
if sudo nixos-rebuild dry-build --flake .#vexos; then
  PASS "nixos-rebuild dry-build"
else
  FAIL "nixos-rebuild dry-build — system closure build failed"
fi

# ---------- CHECK 3: hardware-configuration.nix not tracked --------------
echo "[3/8] Checking hardware-configuration.nix is not tracked..."
if [ -z "$(git ls-files hardware-configuration.nix)" ]; then
  PASS "hardware-configuration.nix not tracked in git"
else
  FAIL "hardware-configuration.nix is tracked in git — remove it immediately"
fi

# ---------- CHECK 4: system.stateVersion present -------------------------
echo "[4/8] Verifying system.stateVersion in configuration.nix..."
if grep -q 'system\.stateVersion' configuration.nix; then
  PASS "system.stateVersion present in configuration.nix"
else
  FAIL "system.stateVersion missing from configuration.nix"
fi

# ---------- Early exit if any hard check failed --------------------------
if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo ""
  echo -e "${RED}Preflight FAILED — resolve hard failures before pushing.${RESET}"
  exit "$EXIT_CODE"
fi

# ---------- CHECK 5: flake.lock freshness (WARN) -------------------------
echo "[5/8] Checking flake.lock freshness..."
# ... (age calculation logic per spec §6.5)

# ---------- CHECK 6: Nix formatting (WARN) -------------------------------
echo "[6/8] Checking Nix formatting..."
# ... (nixpkgs-fmt --check logic per spec §6.6)

# ---------- CHECK 7: No hardcoded secrets (WARN) -------------------------
echo "[7/8] Scanning for hardcoded secrets..."
# ... (grep pattern logic per spec §6.7)

# ---------- CHECK 8: flake.lock committed (WARN) -------------------------
echo "[8/8] Verifying flake.lock is committed..."
# ... (git ls-files check per spec §6.8)

# ---------- Summary -------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "========================================================"
if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo -e "${GREEN}Preflight PASSED — safe to push.${RESET}"
else
  echo -e "${RED}Preflight FAILED — resolve issues before pushing.${RESET}"
fi
echo "========================================================"
echo ""

exit "$EXIT_CODE"
```

---

## 8. Implementation Requirements Summary

| # | Check | Severity | Exit on Fail |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `nix flake check` | HARD | Yes — `exit 1` |
| 2 | `sudo nixos-rebuild dry-build --flake .#vexos` | HARD | Yes — `exit 1` |
| 3 | `hardware-configuration.nix` not tracked in git | HARD | Yes — `exit 1` |
| 4 | `system.stateVersion` present in `configuration.nix` | HARD | Yes — `exit 1` |
| 5 | `flake.lock` updated within 30 days | WARN | No |
| 6 | `nixpkgs-fmt --check .` (if available) | WARN | No |
| 7 | No hardcoded secret patterns in tracked `.nix` files | WARN | No |
| 8 | `flake.lock` exists and is committed | WARN | No |

---

## 9. Early Exit Strategy

Checks 1–4 are grouped as hard failures. After check 4, the script evaluates `$EXIT_CODE`. If any hard check has set it to `1`, the script prints a failure summary and calls `exit 1` immediately — without running warn-only checks. This prevents confusing output from checks 5–8 when the build is fundamentally broken.

---

## 10. File Placement and Permissions

```
scripts/
  preflight.sh    ← executable, committed to repo
```

The implementation subagent must ensure:
- `scripts/` directory is created if it does not exist
- `scripts/preflight.sh` is created with the exact shebang `#!/usr/bin/env bash`
- File permissions are set to executable: `chmod +x scripts/preflight.sh`
- The file is committed as part of the same feature branch

---

## 11. Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| `nixos-rebuild dry-build` requires sudo, may prompt for password | Document in README that preflight requires sudo; CI can use `NOPASSWD` sudoers rule |
| `nixpkgs-fmt` not installed | Check 6 degrades gracefully to WARN if binary absent |
| False positives in secret scan | Patterns are conservative; Check 7 is WARN-only, not HARD |
| `date -d` not available (non-GNU) | NixOS ships GNU coreutils; macOS compat is out of scope |
| Running outside repo root | `git ls-files` and `grep` paths are relative; script must document root requirement |
| Flake lock check redundant with Check 1 | Retained for explicitness and actionable messaging |

---

1. NixOS Manual — nixos-rebuild options: https://nixos.org/manual/nixos/stable/#sec-nixos-rebuild

3. nixpkgs-fmt project: https://github.com/nix-community/nixpkgs-fmt
4. Nix Flakes — lock file semantics: https://nixos.wiki/wiki/Flakes#Lock_files
5. OWASP Secrets Management — hardcoded credential detection patterns
6. Git porcelain commands — `git ls-files` documentation: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-ls-files
